engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is checking out a physical venue — rooms, dining, wedding spaces, history, location/parking — where photos, directions and the feel of the place ARE the decision. No single defined-expertise pitch or portfolio spine.

goal.one_action: book a stay / enquire about a wedding or event (multiple parallel conversion paths)
goal.delegated_to: book-secure.com (Little Hotelier), resdiary, calendly, plus on-site enquiry forms

structure.archetype_note: Multi-facet hospitality venue site: one landing per revenue stream (stay/dine/weddings/celebrations/meetings), each teeing into an external booking engine or an enquiry form.

mapped.observed_as: Wedding Open Day (Sunday 26th July) promo banner
Stay / Dine / Celebrate / Meetings at The Moonraker teasers
22 Unique Rooms to Choose From (room types)
Charming Ceremonies / Wedding Breakfasts / Bridal Suite (wedding facilities)
The History of The Moonraker (Domesday estate to Moonraker)
Enquire About Booking / Tell Us About Your Plans / BOOK A TOUR
How to Find Us (address, parking, train stations)

proposed_new.name: external-booking-redirect
proposed_new.evidence: Every primary booking CTA hands off to a third-party engine — BOOK A STAY / CHECK AVAILABILITY to book-secure.com (Little Hotelier), BOOK A TABLE to resdiary.com, BOOK HERE to Calendly; no on-site scheduling/inventory.

confidence.why: fetch_quality=full, 6 pages crawled with sections, CTAs, embeds and pricing captured; only form field lists are approximate (noted in oddities), which does not affect classification.

gap_findings: Multi-conversion hospitality venue: one site simultaneously drives room bookings (Little Hotelier redirect), table reservations (resdiary redirect), event-open-day (Calendly), and wedding/enquiry forms — the single-one-action ICP frame cannot express a venue with parallel revenue streams each with its own conversion mechanism.
Room-type grid with per-type 'Check Availability' handoff to an external hotel booking engine is not representable by current capability vocab except via the proposed external-booking-redirect.
A repeated cross-page dated promo/event banner (Wedding Open Day) is a recurring section pattern with no canonical section id (mapped to miscellaneous).
